description.step_1: Identify the matrix in question. Eigen values and vectors pertain to a square matrix, which means it has the same number of rows as columns.

description.step_2: To find an eigen vector 'v' for each eigenvalue 'λ', you need to solve the characteristic equation det(A - λI) = 0. This determinant will give a polynomial whose roots are the eigenvalues of matrix A.

description.step_3: With an eigenvalue, plug it back into the original equation (Av = λv). Solve this homogenous system to find corresponding non-zero vectors 'v', which will be your eigenvectors for that particular eigenvalue.
